title: Neurodiagnostic Technologists
description: Conduct electroneurodiagnostic (END) tests such as electroencephalograms, evoked potentials, polysomnograms, or electronystagmograms. May perform nerve conduction studies.

tasks.text: Indicate artifacts or interferences derived from sources outside of the brain, such as poor electrode contact or patient movement, on electroneurodiagnostic recordings.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor patients during tests or surgeries, using electroencephalographs (EEG), evoked potential (EP) instruments, or video recording equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Conduct tests or studies such as electroencephalography (EEG), polysomnography (PSG), nerve conduction studies (NCS), electromyography (EMG), and intraoperative monitoring (IOM).
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Collect patients' medical information needed to customize tests.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Explain testing procedures to patients, answering questions or reassuring patients, as needed.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Set up, program, or record montages or electrical combinations when testing peripheral nerve, spinal cord, subcortical, or cortical responses.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Summarize technical data to assist physicians to diagnose brain, sleep, or nervous system disorders.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct tests to determine cerebral death, the absence of brain activity, or the probability of recovery from a coma.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Attach electrodes to patients, using adhesives.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Measure patients' body parts and mark locations where electrodes are to be placed.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Submit reports to physicians summarizing test results.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Calibrate, troubleshoot, or repair equipment and correct malfunctions, as needed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Adjust equipment to optimize viewing of the nervous system.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Measure visual, auditory, or somatosensory evoked potentials (EPs) to determine responses to stimuli.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Assist in training technicians, medical students, residents, or other staff members.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Participate in research projects, conferences, or technical meetings.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
